author: appfolder

title: [twitter] Add support for x.com domains (Fixes #33267)
body:
Resolves #33267

### Description
Since Twitter's rebranding to X, millions of URLs now use the `x.com` domain. The `_BASE_REGEX` in the Twitter extractor was previously hardcoded to only match `twitter\.com`, causing all `x.com` links to fail before extraction could begin. 

This PR updates the base regular expression to support both domains simultaneously. 

### Technical Details
* Replaced the hardcoded domain literal with a non-capturing alternation group `(?:twitter|x)`.
* Subdomain handling (`www.`, `m.`, `mobile.`) remains unchanged and applies properly to both domains.
* This is a regression-safe, backward-compatible fix that slots directly into the existing structure without modifying downstream `_VALID_URL` consumers.

### Regex Coverage Matrix
| URL | Matches |
|---|---|
| `https://twitter.com/user/status/123` | ✅ |
| `https://mobile.twitter.com/user/status/123` | ✅ |
| `https://x.com/user/status/123` | ✅ |
| `https://mobile.x.com/user/status/123` | ✅ |

Supersedes, closes #32988.
author: adityakrmishra

title: [core] Show [extractor] and video ID in ERROR messages
body:
Fixes #33254

## Summary

Restructures the `ExtractorError` pipeline so that every error message is automatically prefixed with the originating extractor name and video ID:

`ERROR: [youtube] BaW_jenozKc: requested format not available`

Previously, batch downloads with `--ignore-errors` produced unattributed error lines with no indication of which URL triggered them. This change closes the functional gap between `youtube-dl` and `yt-dlp` for this specific output.

---

## Implementation Details

### `youtube_dl/utils.py`
* **`YoutubeDLError`**: Added `msg = None` class attribute and an `__init__` that sets `self.msg`, establishing a uniform interface across the exception hierarchy.
* **`ExtractorError`**: Added `ie` parameter. The formatted message string is no longer assembled eagerly; it is produced by a private `__msg()` method that composes `[ie] video_id: orig_msg` from instance attributes. A custom `__setattr__` keeps `self.msg` and `self.args` in sync whenever a source attribute is assigned after construction.

### `youtube_dl/extractor/common.py`
* Upgraded the bare `except ExtractorError: raise` block in `InfoExtractor.extract()` to catch the exception and backfill missing context before re-raising. This acts as a zero-refactor catch-all: every extractor that raises `ExtractorError` without supplying `ie=` or `video_id=` receives those fields automatically.

### `youtube_dl/extractor/youtube.py`
* The playability-error path (premieres, geo-blocks, login-required) now passes `video_id=video_id` explicitly when raising an `ExtractorError`.

### `youtube_dl/YoutubeDL.py`
* Added `YoutubeDL.raise_no_formats(info, forced=False, **kw_msg)` to centralize format-availability error handling.
* Replaced four bare `raise ExtractorError(...)` call sites in `process_video_result` with `self.raise_no_formats(...)`.

---

## Improvements over the Original Issue Proposal

Three architectural safeguards were added to the original draft patch to ensure stability:

1. **`KeyError` Prevention (`YoutubeDL.py`)**: Changed `info['id']` to `info.get('id')` inside `raise_no_formats`. On the `'Missing "id" field'` code path, `'id'` is absent by definition, which would have triggered a fatal `KeyError` before the intended error could raise.
2. **Scope Correction (`youtube.py`)**: Passed `video_id=video_id` directly to the existing `raise ExtractorError` call instead of incorrectly attempting to call `self.raise_no_formats()` from within `YoutubeIE._real_extract` (as the method exists on `YoutubeDL`, not `InfoExtractor`).
3. **Recursion Guard (`utils.py`)**: Added `name not in ('msg', 'args')` to the custom `__setattr__` in `ExtractorError` to prevent infinite recursion when syncing attributes.

---

## Testing

* **New Tests:** Added `TestExtractorErrorFormatting` class to `test/test_YoutubeDL.py` covering prefix behavior, missing arguments, post-construction attribute assignment, and context backfilling.
* **Backward Compatibility:** All existing `assertRaises(ExtractorError, ...)` calls remain unmodified and pass, as they assert only on exception type. 
* **Verified:** Manual CLI test confirms the new output format works seamlessly with standard command-line flags.
author: adityakrmishra

title: [abematv] Add extractor for Abema TV (yt-dlp backport)
body:
Fixes #33002 

**Note: Opening as a Draft PR. I need someone with a clean, residential Japanese IP address to verify the final fragment download, as my datacenter/VPN IPs are being hard-blocked (403) by Akamai.**

## Summary

This PR introduces an extractor for Abema TV (`abema.tv`), backporting the core logic, device registration, and custom DRM license handling from `yt-dlp` into `youtube-dl`'s older architecture. 

---

## Implementation Details & Architectural Adaptations

Because `yt-dlp` features a significantly different networking and utility layer, several custom adaptations were required to make this work natively in `youtube-dl`:

### 1. `abematv-license://` Scheme Interception
AbemaTV HLS manifests embed `EXT-X-KEY` URIs with a custom scheme. 
* **yt-dlp approach:** Uses a `RequestHandler` plugin system.
* **youtube-dl backport:** Implemented `_AbemaTVLicenseHandler` as a `urllib.BaseHandler` subclass. It is dynamically injected into the `_downloader._opener` during the first `_get_device_token()` call (with an idempotent guard to prevent duplicate handler registration).

### 2. Missing Crypto Primitives Polyfilled
`yt-dlp` relies on several utility functions that do not exist in `youtube-dl`'s `utils.py` or `aes.py`. These were implemented inline:
* `_aes_ecb_decrypt`: Built using the existing `aes_decrypt` and `key_expansion`.
* `_decode_base_n`: Pure arithmetic base conversion.
* `_time_seconds`: Re-implemented to support hour/minute/second offset calculations.

### 3. Akamai CDN Header Enforcements
In `youtube-dl`, `_extract_m3u8_formats` does not automatically inherit session headers. The Akamai CDN serving `vod-abematv.akamaized.net` rigorously enforces API/manifest consistency. To prevent 403s before the DRM phase:
* Passed the required `Authorization: Bearer <device_token>` to `_extract_m3u8_formats`.
* Hardcoded an Android `User-Agent` (`abema-android/3.27.1`) to match the `osName=android` and `appId=tv.abema` used during the API device registration. (Using `youtube-dl`'s default desktop Chrome UA on an Android-registered JWT results in a 403).
* Included `Origin` and `Referer` headers.

---

## Testing

* **Crypto & Logic:** Verified locally. `_aes_ecb_decrypt`, `_decode_base_n`, and `_generate_aks` all output the correct byte sequences and tokens.
* **Network Execution:** Tested via a Tokyo VPN. The device registers successfully, the API returns the playability status, and the `[abematv] Authorizing` step completes flawlessly. 

**The Blocker:** My VPN's IP subnet is blacklisted by Akamai's WAF. The script crashes with an `HTTP Error 403: Forbidden` when fetching the `.m3u8` manifest. 

Could a maintainer or community member with a Japanese residential IP pull this branch and run a test to confirm the `abematv-license://` fragments decrypt correctly?

title: Trim repo to a personal-use YouTube-only CLI
body:
Removes ~147k lines of code that's not needed for downloading YouTube videos from a source checkout on macOS.

Extractors:
- Delete 806 non-YouTube extractor modules; rewrite extractors.py to expose only the YouTube IE classes; drop GenericIE plumbing from __init__.py.

Modules removed:
- update.py + --update flag (use git pull instead)
- swfinterp.py and aes.py (no remaining importers)
- traversal.py (15-line re-export stub of utils.py functions)
- casefold.py (Py2 polyfill)
- version.py (inlined the version string)
- socks.py + all SOCKS proxy plumbing in utils.py / YoutubeDL.py
- downloader/{f4m,ism,rtmp,rtsp,niconico}.py — protocols YouTube doesn't use; inline the two F4M helpers common.py still references

CLI flags removed (with their __init__.py / YoutubeDL.py plumbing):
- --update / -U
- --force-generic-extractor (no GenericIE)
- --cn-verification-proxy (deprecated alias)
- --include-ads (no consumers)
- Adobe Pass: --ap-mso, --ap-username, --ap-password, --ap-list-mso

compat.py: simplified from 3886 to 408 lines by collapsing every Py2/Py3.x try/except fallback to the Py3.12 path; deleted the 2234- line html5 entity polyfill, the Py2.6 xpath reimplementation, and the _workaround_optparse_bug9161 patcher.

utils.py: removed 8 dead helpers (bool_or_none, bytes_to_intlist, decode_packed_codes, decode_png, get_element_by_id, month_by_abbreviation, random_birthday, urlhandle_detect_ext) plus the SOCKS-related code paths.

Repo hygiene:
- Deleted .github/ (CI + issue/PR templates), CONTRIBUTING.md, AUTHORS, ChangeLog, Makefile, tox.ini, MANIFEST.in, setup.py, setup.cfg, bin/youtube-dl, youtube-dl.plugin.zsh, docs/, devscripts/, LICENSE.
- Trimmed README.md to CLI-reference-only sections; removed dead refs to deleted flags / extractors / upstream URLs.
- __main__.py: dropped sys.frozen check and __package__ sys.path fixup.

Verified: 69 local tests pass (test_YoutubeDL, test_compat, test_InfoExtractor, test_cache, test_traversal, test_utils, test_options); python3 -m youtube_dl --version works.

title: Fix case-sensitivity bugs when handling uppercase file extensions
body: This pull request fixes several case-sensitivity bugs across youtube-dl when handling uppercase file extensions (e.g. .MP4, .MP3, .WEBM). Specifically: 1. In utils.py: fixed determine_ext, _change_extension, and _UnsafeExtensionError.sanitize_extension to do case-insensitive comparisons on extensions. 2. In YoutubeDL.py: fixed compatible_formats and correct_ext (in process_info) to check extensions case-insensitively, preventing the creation of double-extension output files (e.g. filename.MP4.mp4). 3. In postprocessors: updated embedthumbnail.py and ffmpeg.py to match extension types case-insensitively, resolving crashes and postprocessing errors when running on files with uppercase extensions. All new and existing unit tests pass successfully.
author: aoright
